questions.question: Regarding micturition reflex, consider the following statements: 1. It is a spinal reflex coordinated by the sacral region of the spinal cord. 2. The internal urethral sphincter is under voluntary control. 3. Higher brain centers can override the reflex temporarily. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1: Correct Micturition (urination) is primarily a spinal reflex. When the urinary bladder gradually fills with urine, stretch receptors present in the bladder wall become activated due to the increasing pressure. These receptors send sensory signals to the sacral region of the spinal cord (S2–S4). In response, parasympathetic nerve impulses are generated that cause contraction of the detrusor muscle of the bladder and relaxation of the internal urethral sphincter. This coordinated activity allows urine to be expelled from the bladder. Therefore, the basic coordination of the micturition reflex occurs at the sacral spinal cord level. Statement 2: Incorrect The internal urethral sphincter is composed of smooth muscle, which means it functions under involuntary (autonomic) control and cannot be consciously controlled. Voluntary control over urination is actually exerted through the external urethral sphincter, which is made of skeletal muscle. Because skeletal muscles are under conscious control, a person can voluntarily contract the external sphincter and temporarily delay urination. Statement 3: Correct Although the micturition reflex is coordinated by the spinal cord, it is also influenced by higher brain centers, particularly regions in the pons and the cerebral cortex. These centers can temporarily suppress or facilitate the reflex, allowing a person to delay urination until it is socially appropriate. However, if the bladder becomes excessively full, the strength of the reflex may eventually override voluntary control, leading to the initiation of micturition.

questions.question: The main constituent of vinegar is:
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: B) CH₃COOH Other acids and their sources: Acid Sources Acetic Acid Vinegar Citric Acid Citrus Fruits(lemon, orange) Tartaric Acid Tamarind, Grapes Oxalic Acid Tomato Lactic Acid Curd Ascorbic Acid Citrus Fruits, Amla Formic Acid Ant Sting, Nettle Sting Butyric Acid Rancid Butter Stearic Acid Oil, Fats Malic Acid Apple, Banana Lauric Acid Coconut milk Euric Acid Mustard

questions.question: The Northern Plains are characterized by a distinct zonation from north to south, starting with the Bhabar belt. Which of the following correctly describes the primary reason for the formation of the Bhabar zone and its hydrological implications?
options: It is a marshy region due to excessive rainfall, leading to high groundwater recharge.
It consists of porous pebble-studded deposits where rivers disappear underground, causing a dry surface with limited agriculture.
It is formed by fine silt deposition, making it ideal for rice cultivation but prone to flooding.
It represents old alluvial soil with kankar nodules, supporting intensive horticulture.
questions.explanation: The Bhabar is the northernmost narrow belt (8–16 km wide) of the Northern Plains, running parallel to the Shiwalik foothills. Formation: Rivers descending from the Himalayas carry heavy loads of coarse materials (pebbles, boulders, gravel, and coarse sand). At the sudden break in slope (where mountains meet plains), these heavy sediments are deposited first, forming alluvial fans that merge into the Bhabar belt. Key characteristic — Porosity: The deposits are highly porous and permeable due to large-sized particles (pebbles and boulders). This allows rainwater and river water to percolate quickly underground → rivers and streams "disappear" (flow subsurface) in this zone, except during heavy monsoons. Hydrological implications: Surface remains mostly dry (no standing water or perennial streams visible), leading to poor surface water availability. Groundwater is recharged well (good aquifers below), but the surface is unsuitable for intensive agriculture — only deep-rooted trees grow here; cultivation is limited.

questions.question: With reference to the Dholera Special Investment Region (Dholera SIR), consider the following statements: 1. It is being developed as India’s first greenfield smart industrial city under the Delhi–Mumbai Industrial Corridor (DMIC) project. 2. It has been designated as a Special Economic Zone (SEZ) under the SEZ Act, 2005. 3. A greenfield international airport is being developed to enhance global connectivity to the region. 4. The region has recently attracted investments in semiconductor and data centre infrastructure. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1: Correct Dholera Special Investment Region is being developed as India’s first greenfield smart industrial city under the Delhi–Mumbai Industrial Corridor project. It is planned as a global manufacturing and investment destination. Statement 2: Incorrect • Dholera has not been designated as a Special Economic Zone under the Special Economic Zone Act, 2005. • It is a Special Investment Region under the Gujarat Special Investment Region Act, 2009. • A Special Economic Zone is primarily an export-oriented tax incentive zone, whereas a Special Investment Region is a large integrated industrial and urban development area. Statement 3: Correct A greenfield international airport is being developed to enhance global connectivity to the region, facilitating both passenger and cargo movement. Statement 4: Correct • The region has recently attracted investments in semiconductor and data centre infrastructure. • Additionally, government provisions have been made for the development of trunk infrastructure.

questions.question: Under whose leadership was the attempt made to establish the Provisional Government of India in Kabul in 1915?
questions.explanation: The attempt to establish the Provisional Government of India in Kabul in 1915 was led by Raja Mahendra Pratap. It was a government-in-exile formed on 1 December 1915 in Kabul, Afghanistan, during the First World War. This effort was supported by the Indian Independence Committee (Berlin Committee) as well as representatives from Germany and Turkey. Its objective was to secure international support against British rule in India. The key positions in this provisional government were as follows— • Raja Mahendra Pratap — President • Maulana Barkatullah — Prime Minister • Ubaidullah Sindhi — Home Minister • Maulavi Bashir — War Minister • Champakraman Pillai — Foreign Affairs Minister The government functioned for a short period and was dissolved around 1919 due to British pressure on Afghanistan. It was distinct from the Provisional Government of Free India established by Subhas Chandra Bose in 1943. Explanation of other options: Subhas Chandra Bose — He was associated with the Provisional Government of Free India established in 1943, not the 1915 Kabul effort. Sachindra Nath Sanyal — He was a revolutionary associated with the Hindustan Republican Association and the Kakori case, not with this Kabul government. Aurobindo Ghosh — He was involved in early revolutionary activities but withdrew from active politics around 1910 and turned towards spiritual pursuits.

questions.question: Laws related to what are provided protection under the Ninth Schedule of the Constitution?
options: Land reforms and certain socio-economic measures(from judicial review)
Tribal areas in Assam, Meghalaya, etc.
questions.explanation: Land reforms and certain socio-economic measures (from judicial review) Schedule 7-12 in details- Seventh Schedule • Related to Article 246. • Division of legislative powers between the Union and States: Union List (central subjects), State List, and Concurrent List. Eighth Schedule • Related to Articles 344(1) and 351. • List of 22 officially recognized languages (originally 14; expanded via amendments like Sindhi, Konkani, Manipuri, Nepali, etc.). Ninth Schedule • Related to Article 31B. • Validation of certain Acts and Regulations (mainly related to land reforms and other socio-economic laws; provides protection from judicial review, though limited by later judgments). Tenth Schedule • Related to Articles 102(2) and 191(2). • Provisions as to disqualification on ground of defection (Anti-Defection Law for MPs and MLAs). Eleventh Schedule • Related to Article 243G (added by 73rd Amendment Act, 1992). • Powers, authority, and responsibilities of Panchayats (rural local self-government; lists 29 subjects like agriculture, rural housing, drinking water, etc.). Twelfth Schedule • Related to Article 243W (added by 74th Amendment Act, 1992). • Powers, authority, and responsibilities of Municipalities (urban local self-government; lists 18 subjects like urban planning, water supply, public health, slum improvement, etc.).

questions.question: Where is the headquarters of the United Nations Development Programme located?
questions.explanation: The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) is headquartered in New York City, USA. It operates under the UN system and works closely with the UN Secretariat, which is also based in New York. Additional information: Geneva (Switzerland) → Hosts many UN bodies like WHO, WTO, ILO, but not UNDP HQ. Paris (France) → Headquarters of UNESCO and OECD. Vienna (Austria) → Hosts IAEA and UNODC.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a feature of expansionary fiscal policy?
questions.explanation: Expansionary fiscal policy is used by the government (typically during a recession or slow economic growth) to stimulate the economy by increasing aggregate demand (AD). This helps boost output, employment, and growth. The main tools/characteristics of expansionary fiscal policy are: • Increase in government spending (G ↑) → directly increases AD. • Decrease in personal taxes → increases disposable income → increases consumption (C ↑) → increases AD. • Decrease in business taxes (corporate taxes) → increases after-tax profits → encourages investment (I ↑) → increases AD. Why is a decrease in government spending wrong? Decrease in government spending is the opposite — it reduces government purchases, lowers AD, and is actually a feature of contractionary fiscal policy (used to cool down an overheating economy or fight inflation).

questions.question: Why is it generally more severe to be burnt by steam at 100°C than by boiling water at 100°C?
options: Steam is hotter than boiling water.
Steam contains more latent heat of vaporization.
Steam has a higher specific heat capacity.
questions.explanation: Both steam (water vapour) at 100°C and boiling water (liquid) at 100°C have the same temperature. Temperature reflects the average kinetic energy of molecules, so the molecules in both steam and boiling water possess the same average kinetic energy at this temperature. Therefore, steam is not hotter than boiling water, and the molecular motion is not faster simply because it is in the gaseous state. Option A is incorrect because steam is not hotter than boiling water; both are exactly at 100°C under normal atmospheric pressure. Option C is incorrect because steam does not have a higher specific heat capacity than liquid water. In fact, the specific heat capacity of liquid water (about 4.18 J/g°C) is higher than that of steam (about 2.01 J/g°C). Option D is also incorrect because, at the same temperature, the average kinetic energy of molecules remains the same, so steam molecules are not moving faster than those in boiling water due solely to temperature. Option B is correct because steam contains additional energy in the form of latent heat of vaporization. When steam at 100°C comes into contact with cooler skin, it first condenses into liquid water at 100°C. During this phase change, steam releases a large amount of latent heat (about 2260 J per gram) that was absorbed when the water originally evaporated. After condensation, the newly formed water then cools from 100°C toward the skin temperature (around 37°C), releasing even more heat. Because of this extra release of latent heat during condensation, steam transfers much more energy to the skin than boiling water at the same temperature, which is why steam causes more severe burns.

questions.question: Which one of the following pairs is correctly matched?
questions.explanation: A) Quicklime – Calcium carbonate. • Quicklime is Calcium oxide (CaO). • Calcium carbonate (CaCO₃) is limestone. So this pair is incorrect. B) Slaked lime – Calcium hydroxide • Slaked lime is Calcium hydroxide (Ca(OH)₂). • It is formed when quicklime (CaO) reacts with water: So this pair is correct. C) Baking soda – Sodium carbonate • Baking soda is Sodium bicarbonate (NaHCO₃). • Sodium carbonate (Na₂CO₃) is washing soda (in hydrated form). So this pair is incorrect. D) Washing soda – Sodium bicarbonate • Washing soda is Sodium carbonate decahydrate (Na₂CO₃·10H₂O). • Sodium bicarbonate is baking soda. So this pair is incorrect.

questions.question: The August Offer was announced by:
questions.explanation: The August Offer was announced in 1940 during World War II. It was declared by the then Viceroy Lord Linlithgow. Key Points of August Offer: • Dominion Status as ultimate goal • Expansion of Viceroy’s Executive Council • Indians to frame a new constitution after the war • No future constitution without minority consent The offer was rejected by Congress because it did not promise immediate responsible government. Important sequence- • 1935 → Provincial Autonomy • 1940 → August Offer • 1942 → Cripps Mission • 1946 → Cabinet Mission

questions.question: Match the following:- Book Author A)India Wins Freedom 1. Maulana Abul Kalam Azad B) The Indian War of Independence 2. Vinayak Damodar Savarkar C) Glimpses of World History 3. Jawaharlal Nehru D) Unhappy India 4. Lala Lajpat Rai
questions.explanation: A) India Wins Freedom → 1. Maulana Abul Kalam Azad (Autobiographical account of India's partition and freedom struggle, written by Maulana Azad, India's first Education Minister and long-time Congress President.) B) The Indian War of Independence → 2. Vinayak Damodar Savarkar (Nationalist interpretation of the 1857 Revolt as India's first War of Independence, written by Veer Savarkar in 1909.) C) Glimpses of World History → 3. Jawaharlal Nehru This is a series of letters on world history that Jawaharlal Nehru wrote to his daughter, Indira, during his imprisonment. The collection of these letters was published in 1934. D) Unhappy India → 4. Lala Lajpat Rai This book was written by Lala Lajpat Rai. It was written as a response to Katherine Mayo's controversial book, *Mother India*, presenting a perspective on Indian society and culture. The book was published in 1928.

questions.question: In the question given below, an assertion (A) is given, followed by a reason(R) which may or may not explain the assertion. From the given options, choose the right answer. Assertion (A): The 1991 reforms completely eliminated the License-Permit-Quota Raj in India. Reason (R): Industrial licensing was abolished for most industries except a few related to security and environmental concerns.
options: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is true, but R is false.
A is false, but R is true.
questions.explanation: Assertion (A) is false: The License-Permit-Quota Raj was not completely eliminated in 1991. The New Industrial Policy of 1991, introduced under Prime Minister P. V. Narasimha Rao and Finance Minister Manmohan Singh, significantly reduced government controls over industries. However, it did not abolish the system entirely. Industrial licensing was removed for most sectors, but some industries continued to require government approval. Strategic sectors remained under strong public sector presence, and regulatory controls persisted in areas such as environmental clearances and foreign investment approvals (initially handled through the Foreign Investment Promotion Board). In addition, several products remained reserved for the small-scale industry sector for many years. Therefore, it is incorrect to say that the License-Permit-Quota Raj was completely eliminated in 1991. Reason (R) is true: The Industrial Policy of 1991 abolished industrial licensing for most industries in order to promote liberalization and encourage private sector participation. However, licensing requirements were retained for a limited number of sectors considered strategically important or sensitive. These included industries related to national security and defence, the production of hazardous chemicals, and certain environment-sensitive industries. Thus, the reason statement correctly describes the partial removal of industrial licensing under the 1991 reforms. Additional Information: License-Permit-Quota Raj Before 1991, India followed a state-led planned development model influenced by socialist economic principles. Under this system, industries needed government permission to start new units, expand capacity, or change production lines. Production capacity was tightly regulated, and imports were controlled through quotas and high tariffs. Large business houses faced expansion limits under the Monopolies and Restrictive Trade Practices framework, while the public sector dominated many core industries. This regulatory system developed mainly after the enactment of the Industries (Development and Regulation) Act, 1951 and became widely known as the License-Permit-Quota Raj.

questions.question: The 6th National Rafting Championship was titled:
questions.explanation: Correct answer: A) The Ultimate River Battle It took place from February 19 to 22, 2026, on the Chenab River at Shibnote (Premnagar) in the Doda district. The event was organized by the Directorate of Tourism Jammu in collaboration with the Doda district administration to promote adventure tourism and showcase rafting talent from across the country.

questions.question: The male reproductive part of a flower is called:
questions.explanation: In a typical flower, the male reproductive organ is called the stamen. The stamen has two main parts: • Anther – Produces pollen grains (which contain male gametes). • Filament – A thin stalk that supports the anther. Other options: A) Pistil – It is the female reproductive part of the flower. B) Ovule – It is present inside the ovary and contains the female gamete. D) Sepal – It protects the flower in the bud stage; it is not a reproductive part.

questions.question: The fiscal deficit for FY 2026-27 was targeted at what percentage of GDP in the Union Budget 2026-27?
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: B) 4.3 % fiscal deficit:- • The fiscal deficit is the gap between what the government spends and what it earns (excluding borrowings). • A 4.3 % fiscal deficit means the government plans to spend money that is equal to 4.3 % of India’s GDP more than its revenue (not including loans) in the financial year 2026-27. Importantance:- • It shows how much the government needs to borrow to fund its development programmes, welfare schemes, and other expenditures after accounting for revenues. • A target of 4.3 % of GDP indicates a continued focus on fiscal discipline while still supporting growth — slightly lower than the revised estimate of 4.4 % in the previous year.

questions.question: With reference to Harshavardhana, consider the following statements: • He was the last great ruler of ancient India before the Delhi Sultanate. • He convened a grand religious assembly at Kannauj. • His empire included the entire Deccan region. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1: Correct • Harshavardhana (606–647 CE) is generally regarded as the last powerful ruler of North India in the ancient period before large-scale political fragmentation. • After his death, North India saw the rise of regional powers like the Palas, Pratiharas, and Rashtrakutas. • The next major phase of political unification in North India came much later with the Delhi Sultanate (1206 CE onward). Statement 2: Correct • Harsha organized a grand religious assembly at Kannauj. • According to the Chinese traveler Xuanzang, Harsha presided over this assembly where Buddhist doctrines were discussed and promoted. • He also held religious gatherings at Prayaga (Allahabad). Statement 3: Incorrect Harsha attempted to expand into the Deccan but was defeated by Pulakeshin II of the Chalukyas. Therefore, his empire did not include the entire Deccan region. His effective control was mainly over North India.

questions.question: Which of the following best reflects the fundamental objective of applying generative design in aerospace systems engineering?
options: Facilitating seamless integration of multi-payload platforms in shared bus structures
Optimizing mechanical configurations for minimum mass and maximum structural resilience
Enhancing life-support redundancies to meet crew safety protocols in deep-space missions
Designing adaptive thrust mechanisms to dynamically control orbital corrections
questions.explanation: In Aviation and Space Systems Engineering, the primary objective of generative design is the automated generation of various design alternatives based on specified constraints. These limitations include: • Load conditions • Material properties • Manufacturing methods • Weight limits • Performance requirements The core objective in aerospace is almost always: Reduce mass while maintaining or improving structural strength and performance. Because in aerospace: • Lower mass → lower fuel consumption • Lower mass → higher payload efficiency • Lower mass → improved mission economics Generative design produces organic, optimized structures that remove unnecessary material while preserving load-bearing capacity.

questions.question: With reference to the administrative system of the Bahamani rulers, consider the following statements: • The kingdom was divided into ‘Tarafs’ governed by Tarafdars. • The Tarafdars enjoyed complete hereditary control without interference from the Sultan. • Mahmud Gawan attempted to curb the power of the Tarafdars through administrative reforms. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1: Correct • The Bahamani Kingdom divided its territory into ‘Tarafs’ (provinces). • Each taraf was administered by an official called a Tarafdar. • This system was somewhat similar to the iqta system but evolved in the Deccan context. Statement 2: Incorrect Tarafdars were powerful provincial governors, but they did not enjoy complete hereditary and unchecked authority. In fact: • Their growing autonomy became a political problem. • The Sultan retained the right to transfer or remove them. • Excessive power of Tarafdars led to internal instability. So, saying they had complete hereditary control without interference is incorrect. Statement 3: Correct Mahmud Gawan, a famous Bahamani vizier, introduced reforms to: • Reduce the size of large Tarafs • Increase the number of provinces • Ensure revenue directly reached the central treasury • Curtail the excessive power of provincial nobles His reforms were meant to strengthen central authority and reduce factional conflicts.

questions.question: Consider the following features: 1. Roche moutonnée 2. Drumlin 3. Esker 4. Outwash plain Which of the above are primarily depositional landforms?
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: B) 2, 3 and 4 only 1. Roche moutonnée: This is a classic glacial erosional landform. It forms from bedrock sculpted by glacial abrasion (on the stoss/upstream side, creating a smooth, polished surface) and plucking/quarrying (on the lee/downstream side, creating a steeper, jagged face). No significant deposition is involved; it's carved from existing rock. 2. Drumlin: This is a depositional landform. Drumlins are streamlined, elongated hills primarily composed of till (glacial sediment) or other materials reshaped and deposited beneath moving ice. They build up through glacial deposition processes, though some debate exists on exact mechanisms. 3. Esker: This is a depositional landform. Eskers are long, sinuous ridges of stratified sand and gravel deposited by meltwater streams flowing in tunnels within or beneath the glacier (subglacial/englacial channels). 4. Outwash plain: This is a depositional landform. It consists of broad, flat areas of sorted sand, gravel, and silt deposited by meltwater streams flowing from the glacier's snout, often forming layered (stratified) plains beyond the ice margin.

questions.question: In the question given below, an assertion (A) is given, followed by a reason(R) which may or may not explain the assertion. From the given options, choose the right answer. Assertion (A): Parliament can increase the jurisdiction of the Supreme Court but cannot curtail its powers under judicial review. Reason (R): Judicial review is part of the Basic Structure of the Constitution.
options: Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are correct but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is correct but R is incorrect
A is incorrect but R is correct
questions.explanation: Assertion (A) is correct: Parliament has the authority to increase the jurisdiction of the Supreme Court. Under Article 138 of the Constitution of India, Parliament may enlarge the jurisdiction and powers of the Supreme Court with respect to matters included in the Union List. However, Parliament cannot curtail the Court’s power of judicial review. Judicial review is an essential constitutional function through which the judiciary examines the constitutionality of legislative and executive actions. This power is derived from several constitutional provisions, including Articles 13, 32, 136, and 226, which collectively empower courts to protect fundamental rights and uphold constitutional supremacy. Reason (R) is correct: Judicial review has been recognized by the Supreme Court as part of the Basic Structure of the Constitution, which cannot be altered or destroyed by Parliament. This principle was established in the landmark case of Kesavananda Bharati v. State of Kerala and later reaffirmed in Minerva Mills v. Union of India. According to the Basic Structure doctrine, even the constitutional amendment power under Article 368 cannot be used to damage or eliminate essential features of the Constitution, including judicial review. Since judicial review is part of the Constitution’s basic structure, Parliament cannot curtail or abolish it. Therefore, the Reason correctly explains why Parliament cannot restrict the Supreme Court’s power of judicial review, making both the Assertion and the Reason correct, with the Reason serving as the correct explanation of the Assertion.

questions.question: In 2025, for what discovery were Mary E. Brunckow, Fred Ramsdell, and Shimon Sakaguchi awarded the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine?
questions.explanation: Correct option: D) Peripheral immune tolerance The Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine 2025 was awarded jointly to Mary E. Brunkow, Fred Ramsdell, and Shimon Sakaguchi "for their discoveries concerning peripheral immune tolerance." This refers to their groundbreaking work on regulatory T cells (Tregs)—specialized immune cells that act as "security guards" to suppress excessive immune responses, prevent the immune system from attacking the body's own tissues (self-tolerance), and maintain immune homeostasis. Key contributions include: Shimon Sakaguchi's identification of regulatory T cells and their role in preventing autoimmunity. Mary Brunkow and Fred Ramsdell's discovery of the FOXP3 gene (also called Scurfin), mutations in which cause severe autoimmune diseases (e.g., IPEX syndrome in humans), and its function as the master regulator of Treg development and activity. Linking these to explain peripheral immune tolerance mechanisms beyond central tolerance in the thymus.

questions.question: Arrange the following east coast deltaic systems from north to south: • Godavari • Mahanadi • Krishna • Kaveri Select the correct answer using the code below:
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: A) 2–1–3–4 The east-flowing peninsular rivers of India form deltas where they meet the Bay of Bengal. From north to south, the sequence of these deltaic systems is: Mahanadi (2): Drains eastern Odisha and forms a delta near Cuttack. Godavari (1): Drains parts of Maharashtra, Telangana, and Andhra Pradesh; delta near Rajahmundry. Krishna (3): Drains Maharashtra, Karnataka, Telangana, and Andhra Pradesh; delta near Vijayawada. Kaveri (4): Drains Karnataka and Tamil Nadu; delta near Thanjavur (also called the "Rice Bowl of Tamil Nadu"). Additional information:- 1. Mahanadi Origin: Sihawa hills (Dandakaranya region), Chhattisgarh. Length: ~860–900 km. Flows through: Chhattisgarh and Odisha; empties into Bay of Bengal. Key features: Second-highest water potential among peninsular rivers (after Godavari); famous for Hirakud Dam (India's first major multipurpose project post-independence); forms a large delta in Odisha; often called the "Sorrow of Odisha" due to past floods. 2. Godavari Origin: Trimbakeshwar near Nashik, Maharashtra (Western Ghats). Length: ~1,465 km (second-longest river in India after Ganga). Flows through: Maharashtra, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Odisha; empties into Bay of Bengal. Key features: Largest peninsular river system; known as "Dakshina Ganga" (Ganges of the South); drains ~10% of India's land area; forms a vast fertile delta; vital for agriculture and irrigation. 3. Krishna Origin: Near Mahabaleshwar, Western Ghats, Maharashtra. Length: ~1,300–1,400 km. Flows through: Maharashtra, Karnataka, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh; empties into Bay of Bengal. Key features: Third-longest river in India; second-largest peninsular river after Godavari; forms a broad, fertile delta near Vijayawada; crucial for irrigation, agriculture (rice, sugarcane), and hydropower. 4. Kaveri (Cauvery) Origin: Talakaveri, Brahmagiri hills, Western Ghats, Karnataka. Length: ~800–805 km. Flows through: Karnataka and Tamil Nadu; empties into Bay of Bengal. Key features: Largest river in Tamil Nadu; forms the fertile "Rice Bowl" delta (Cauvery Delta) in Tamil Nadu; highly sacred and culturally significant; supports intensive agriculture but often faces interstate water disputes.

questions.question: Who among the following was the first Indian to win an Academy Award (Oscar)?
questions.explanation: Bhanu Athaiya won the Academy Award (Oscar) for Best Costume Design at the 55th Academy Awards in 1983. She received the award for her work in the film Gandhi, directed by Richard Attenborough. • She became the first Indian citizen to win an Oscar. • Her win marked India's first competitive Academy Award victory. • She designed costumes that authentically recreated the colonial-era setting of India. About the Film: • Gandhi (1982) was a biographical film based on Mahatma Gandhi. • The film won 8 Academy Awards, including Best Picture. other options: • Satyajit Ray received an Honorary Oscar in 1992, but not before 1983. • R. Rahman won Oscars in 2009 for Slumdog Millionaire. • Resul Pookutty also won in 2009 for Best Sound Mixing.

questions.question: Under which of the following circumstances can a joint sitting of Parliament be summoned? • When a Bill is rejected by the Rajya Sabha. • When the Rajya Sabha fails to pass the Bill within six months. • When amendments suggested by the Rajya Sabha are not acceptable to the Lok Sabha. • When a Constitutional Amendment Bill is pending in Rajya Sabha for more than six months. Select the correct answer:
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: A) 1, 2 and 3 only The provision for Joint Sitting is under Article 108 of the Constitution of India. A Joint Sitting can be called by the President when there is a deadlock between the two Houses over an Ordinary Bill (not Money Bill or Constitutional Amendment Bill). Deadlock arises in the following three situations: Statement 1: When a Bill is rejected by the Rajya Sabha. Correct. If the Rajya Sabha rejects a Bill passed by the Lok Sabha, it creates a deadlock. Statement 2: When the Rajya Sabha fails to pass the Bill within six months. Correct. If the Rajya Sabha does not pass the Bill within six months from the date it receives it, this counts as a deadlock. Statement 3: When amendments suggested by the Rajya Sabha are not acceptable to the Lok Sabha. Correct. If both Houses disagree on amendments and cannot resolve the disagreement, a joint sitting may be summoned. Statement 4: When a Constitutional Amendment Bill is pending in Rajya Sabha for more than six months. Incorrect. Article 108 clearly states that Joint Sitting is NOT applicable to: Money Bills Constitutional Amendment Bills (Article 368) A Constitutional Amendment Bill must be passed separately by both Houses with special majority — no joint sitting allowed. Additional information:- Joint sitting applies only to Ordinary Bills. It is presided over by the Speaker of the Lok Sabha. So far, it has been used very rarely (e.g., Dowry Prohibition Bill, Banking Service Commission Repeal Bill, POTA Bill).

questions.question: If a² + b² + c² = 2(3a + 5b + 7c) - 83, find 3(a + c) - 4b

Piyush distributed 90% of his total money between Pritam and Arijit in the ratio of 5:4. Pritam invested his share at 8% p.a. for 4 years and Arijit invested his share at 6% p.a. for 5 years. If the difference in the total amounts received by Pritam and Arijit at simple interest after respective time periods is ₹7000, then what is the total amount Piyush had?
questions.explanation: Let’s assume, Piyush had Rs. 1000x He distributed 90% of 1000x=Rs.900x So, Pritam & Arjit got Rs. 500x & Rs. 400x respectively. Total amount earned by Pritam =(500x+500x×8%×4) =660x Total amount earned by Arijit =(400x+400x×6%×5) =520x ∴ Difference in their respective amounts =(660x-520x)=140x ACQ, 140x=7000 or x=50 So, Piyush had: Rs. 1000x=1000×50= Rs. 50,000

questions.question: The sum of A and B is 200% of the sum of C and D. C is 40% more than D. A is 300% of B. If D is equal to 500, then what is the value of A?
The base of a right prism is a regular hexagon with each side of 10.5 cm. If the height of the prism is 16 cm, calculate the approximate value of the volume of the prism.
A person sells 15 notebooks for ₹600 and suffers a loss equal to the selling price of 3 notebooks. What is the cost price of the one notebook?
questions.explanation: Selling price of 15 notebooks =600Rs. 1 ∴ =40 Rs Total loss is equal to selling price of 3 notebooks =(3×40)Rs =120Rs. So, the cost price =(600+120)Rs=720Rs for all 15 notebooks ∴ Cost Price of one notebooks =720/15 Rs=48Rs

questions.question: A triangle-shaped garden has an area of 108 m2. A similar but smaller garden is built with its side being one-fourth the length of the existing garden’s side. What is the area of the smaller garden?
questions.explanation: According to question → side ratio of the bigger garden & smaller garden =4:1 As Area ∝ (side)², their area ratio will be: 4²:1² =16:1 So, Area of the smaller garden =108/16×1=6.75 cm²

questions.question: A hemispherical shell has an inner diameter of 28 cm and a thickness of 1 cm. Calculate the volume (in cm3) of the material forming the shell.
questions.explanation: Inner radius of the hemispherical shell = 28/2 = 14 cm. As Thickness = 1 cm; outer radius = (14 + 1) = 15 cm. So, volume of the material forming the shell = (2/3) π (15³ - 14³) cm³ = (2 × (3375 - 2744))/3 π cm³ = 1262π/3 cm³

questions.question: The point equidistant from all vertexes of a triangle is called:
questions.explanation: The point equidistant from all vertexes of a triangle is called Circumcenter

questions.question: If a person had moved 8 km/h faster, he would have taken 30 minutes less to travel a certain distance. Find his original speed (in km/h) if he takes 3 hours to travel the distance.
questions.explanation: riginal time needed = 3 hrs Time needed when speed is increased = (3 hrs - 30 min) = 2½ hrs = 5/2 hrs Clearly, Time & speed are inversely related when distance is constant. Time ratio → 3 : 5/2 or, 6:5 So, Speed ratio → 5:6 Let’s say, Original speed = 5x km/h & increased speed = 6x km/h ATQ: 6x - 5x = 8 or, x = 8 ∴ Original speed = 5 × 8 = 40 km/h

questions.question: A and B can complete a work in 15 days while B and C can complete the same work in 20 days. A, B and C together can finish the work in 12 days. How long will A and C together take to complete the work?
A circle is inscribed in ∆ABC, touching AB, BC and AC at the points X, Y and Z, respectively. If AB - BC = 6 cm, AB - AC =2 cm and the perimeter of ∆ABC is 40 cm. Find AZ + BY.
questions.explanation: Tangents drawn from a point outside of a circle to the circle are equal in length. Let’s assume AX = AZ = z cm BX = BY = x cm CY = CZ = y cm So, Perimeter = 2x + 2y + 2z = 40 cm or, x + y + z = 20 cm ATQ, AB - BC = (z + x) - (x + y) = 6 cm or, z - y = 6 cm and AB - AC = (z + x) - (z + y) = 2 cm or, x - y = 2 cm (1) - (ii) → x + 2y = 14 (iv) - (iii) → 3y = 12 or, y = 4; by solving further, x = 6, & z = 10 So, AZ + BY = (z + x) = (10 + 6) = 16 cm

questions.question: A seller marks his products 38% above their cost price and allows a 15% discount for cash payment or a 12% discount for online payment. His percentage of profit when a customer pays in cash is:
questions.explanation: Let’s Assume, Cost Price =100x Rs. ∴ Marked Price =138x Rs. Discount percentage for cash payment =15% ∴SP=138x×(100-15)%=138x×85/180 Rs =117.30xRs. ∴ Profit percentage ={(117.30x-100x)}/100x×100%=17.3%

questions.question: 4 numbers are in arithmetic progression. The sum of the 1st and the last number is 58, what is the average of these 4 numbers?
questions.explanation: When 4 numbers are in AP, (1st no + 4th no)/2 = (2nd no + 3rd no)/2 = Avg. ∴ Average of these 4 numbers = 58/2 = 29

questions.question: A bag contains 9 green balls, 7 white balls and 8 black balls. When one ball is drawn from this bag, what is the probability that it is neither a white nor a black ball?
questions.explanation: Green balls =9; White ball =7; Black ball =8 According to the condition provided in the question - neither a white nor a black ball = Must be a green

questions.question: The number of integer as well as perfect square numbers between 40 and 500 is:
questions.explanation: Perfect square numbers b/w 40 & 500 are → 49, 64, 81, 100, … 484 or, 7², 8², 9², 10², …, 22² ∴ Total number of such numbers ⇒ (22 - 7 + 1) = 16

questions.question: A box contains ₹1, ₹2 and ₹5 coins in the ratio of 2:5:12. The total amount in the box is ₹864. Find the amount contributed by ₹2 coins only.
questions.explanation: Let’s say, Numbers of ₹1,₹2,₹5 coins =2x,5x & 12x respectively. ATQ, Total amount ⇒1×2x+2×5x+5×12x=864 Rs. or, 72x=864 or, x=12 ∴ Amount contributed by ₹2 coins is =(2×5x) Rs. =120Rs.

questions.question: If sin∅+cos∅=√5/2, and ∅ is an acute angle, find the value of sin∅-cos∅.
questions.explanation: sin ϕ + cos ϕ = √5 / 2 or, (sin ϕ + cos ϕ)² = 5/4 or, 1 + 2 sin ϕ ⋅ cos ϕ = 5/4 or, 2 sin ϕ ⋅ cos ϕ = 1/4 Now, (sin ϕ - cos ϕ)² = 1 - 2 sin ϕ ⋅ cos ϕ = 1 - 1/4 = 3/4 ∴ sin ϕ - cos ϕ = ± √3 / 2

questions.question: A line passes through two points: (3,5) and (4, -9). What is the slope of the line?
questions.explanation: Slope of a line = (y₂ - y₁)/(x₂ - x₁) = (5 - (-9))/(3 - 4) = -14

questions.question: What quantity of sugar costing ₹24 per kg must be mixed with 28 kg of sugar costing ₹14 per kg so that the shopkeeper may get 25% profit by selling the mixture at ₹20 per kg?
questions.explanation: 25% profit is earned if SP = ₹20 per kg. So, cost price = 20 × 100/125 = ₹16 per kg​ Let’s say, x kg of sugar @ ₹24 per kg is mixed with 28 kg of sugar @ ₹14 per kg. Now, (x × 24) + 28 × 14 = (x + 28) × 16 or, 8x = 28 × 2 or, x = 7 ∴ Expected Quantity = 7 kg

questions.question: Find the range of the following data: 33, 33, 32, 41, 34, 39, 29, 35, 37, 31
questions.explanation: Range of a data set = difference b/ω the smallest & the largest value =(29∼41)=12

questions.question: What sum will amount to ₹330,088 in 3 years at the rate of 10% p.a. at compound interest compounded annually?
A class of 60 students has P25 = 52 in Hindi examination. How many students scored more than 52?
questions.explanation: P₂₅ means 25th percentile which means 25% of total students scored equal to or less than 52 marks in Hindi. Total students = 60 ∴ P₂₅ = 60 × 25% = 15. So, 15 students scored ⩽ 52 marks. So, students scoring > 52 are (60 - 15) = 45

questions.question: A kite is flying at a height of 75 meters above the ground. The string attached to the kite makes an angle of 60 ̊ with the ground. What is the length of the string?
questions.explanation: Let’s assume, the length of the string = T meters ∴ sin 60° = 75/T or, T = (75 × 2)/√3 or, T = 50√3 m

questions.question: In a business partnership, 3/4th of Amit’s amount is equal to 7/9th of Bidisha’s amount. How much will Bidisha get out of a yearly-profit of ₹4000?
questions.explanation: Let’s assume, Amit's amount = A Rs. & Bidisha's amount = B Rs. ATQ: 3/4 × A = 7/9 × B or, A/B = 28/27 Profit is shared among the partners according to their amount's ratio. ∴ Profit ratio of Amit : Bidisha = 28:27 ∴ Bidisha's share of profit = (27/(28+27) × 4000) Rs = (27/55 × 4000) Rs = 21600/11 Rs ≈ 1963.64 Rs.

questions.question: Find the value of - 642/3 - 27-2/3
The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
options: The seasoned diplomat, whom the Prime Minister
believed was the only person capable of negotiating
the complex maritime treaty, has resigned.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A) 👉 Replace "whom" with "who" 📌 Error concept: This is a classic "Who vs. Whom" error involving an intervening clause (the Prime Minister believed). Grammatical concept: In SSC exams, the most effective way to solve this is the "Subject-Verb Counting" method: Count the Verbs: believed (Verb 1) was (Verb 2) has resigned (Verb 3) Identify their Subjects: the Prime Minister is the subject for believed. The seasoned diplomat is the subject for has resigned. "was" is left without a subject. Since every verb must have a subject, we need the subjective pronoun "who" to act as the subject for the verb was. Who: Used as a Subject (performs the action). Whom: Used as an Object (receives the action).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. To complete the mural, the artist had to ______ her childhood memories of the countryside to capture the right emotions.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (d) 📌 Explanation - Draw on (or draw upon) means to use information, experience, or a resource that you have available to help you do something. In this sentence, the artist is using her "childhood memories" as a resource for her work. Draw on: (किसी स्रोत या अनुभव का) उपयोग करना - To use a resource, experience, or knowledge to help you do something. Example: As a writer, she draws on her experiences living in different countries to create her characters. Draw up: (दस्तावेज़) तैयार करना या बनाना - To prepare a formal document, list, or plan. Example: The legal team was asked to draw up a new contract for the merger. Draw out: किसी चीज़ को लंबा खींचना या किसी को बोलने के लिए प्रोत्साहित करना - To make something last longer than usual, or to encourage someone to be more talkative/expressive. Example: The teacher tried to draw out the shy student by asking simple questions. Draw back: पीछे हटना - To move away from someone or something, usually out of fear, surprise, or a change of mind Example: She started to draw back from the deal when she realized the hidden costs involved.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. The new operating system is not compatible to the older hardware, leading to frequent system crashes and performance issues.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (c) 📌 Explanation - The adjective "compatible" always takes the fixed preposition "with" when expressing that two things can exist or work together without conflict. Using "to," "into," or "for" is grammatically incorrect in this context. Compatible with: अनुकूल होना / सामंजस्यपूर्ण होना - Able to exist or occur together without conflict. Example : Their marriage failed because they were not compatible with each other.

questions.question: Identify the type of the following sentence: Although the rain was heavy and the wind was blowing fiercely, the determined trekkers continued their journey toward the summit.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (c) 📌 Explanation - The sentence contains one Independent Clause ("the determined trekkers continued their journey toward the summit") and one Dependent Clause starting with a subordinating conjunction ("Although the rain was heavy and the wind was blowing fiercely"). Any sentence with one independent and at least one dependent clause is a Complex Sentence. Analysis of other options: (a) Simple: Incorrect because the sentence has more than one subject-verb pair and a subordinating conjunction. (b) Compound: Incorrect because "Although" creates a dependent relationship, not an equal one between two independent thoughts. (d) Compound-Complex: Incorrect because while there is an "and" inside the dependent clause, there are not two separate independent clauses joined by a coordinating conjunction.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. I can't help feel sorry for her, even though she was clearly in the wrong and had made some poor decisions.
options: I can't help but feel sorry for her
I can't feel sorry for her
I can't help and feel sorry for her
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A) 📌 Explanation - The original sentence "I can't help feel sorry" is grammatically incomplete. In English, the expression used to say that one cannot stop themselves from doing or feeling something is "cannot help but + V1" or "cannot help + V-ing." Since "feel" is in the base form (V1), it must be preceded by "but." 📌 Grammatical concept: There are two specific structures used with the verb help when it means "to refrain from" or "to avoid" 1) Subject + cannot help + V-ing: Example: I can't help feeling sorry for her. 2) Subject + cannot help but + V1 (Base form): Example: I can't help but feel sorry for her. Why other options are wrong: Option B: Changes the entire meaning of the sentence from feeling sympathy to not feeling it. Option C: "Help and feel" is logically and grammatically incorrect; "and" implies two separate actions, whereas the idiom is about the inability to avoid one specific feeling. Option D: Incorrect because "help" cannot be followed directly by a base verb (V1) without "but" in this idiomatic context.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. The committee decided to ______ the decision until more information could be gathered from the field reports.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (b). 📌 Explanation - Defer means to put off an action or event to a later time; to postpone. The context of the sentence (waiting for "more information") logically requires a word that means delaying the decision. Meaning of all options: Defer: टालना / स्थगित करना - To delay or put off an action or event to a later time; to postpone. Example: The bank agreed to defer the customer's mortgage payments for three months. Forestall: पहले से ही रोक देना / पूर्वोपाय करना - To prevent or obstruct an anticipated event by taking action ahead of time. Example: The company lowered its prices to forestall a drop in sales. Refute: खंडन करना / गलत सिद्ध करना - To prove a statement, theory, or person to be wrong or false. Example: The lawyer presented evidence to refute the witness's testimony. Renounce: त्याग देना / छोड़ देना - To formally declare one's abandonment of a claim, right, or possession. Example: He was forced to renounce his citizenship to hold a political office in the new country.

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains grammatical error.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C) 👉 Replace "much more cowardly" with "in a much more cowardly manner/way" 📌 Error concept: This is an error of Adjective vs. Adverb usage. Words ending in -ly like cowardly, miserly, friendly, and silly are Adjectives, not Adverbs. Grammatical concept: In English, to modify a verb (handled), we need an Adverb. However, "cowardly" is an Adjective (it describes a noun). You cannot simply add -er or more to an adjective and use it as an adverb. Rules: 1) Nouns + ly = Adjective (e.g., Friend + ly = Friendly; Coward + ly = Cowardly). 2) To use these as adverbs, we must use the phrase: "In a/an + Adjective + manner/way." Incorrect: He fought cowardly. (Adjective used as Adverb) Correct: He fought in a cowardly manner. (Adverbial phrase)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blanks. The critic argued that while the protagonist's actions were purely ___(1)___ as he lacked any sense of right or wrong, the antagonist’s calculated cruelty was undeniably ___(2)___.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (b) 📌 Explanation - Blank 1: The context "lacked any sense of right or wrong" refers to being outside the sphere of morality entirely. This is the definition of Amoral. Blank 2: "Calculated cruelty" implies that the person knows what is right but chooses to do what is wrong. This is the definition of Immoral. Amoral: नीतिहीन / नीति-निरपेक्ष - Lacking a moral sense; unconcerned with the rightness or wrongness of something. (Used for infants, animals, or robots who don't understand the concept of morality). Example: Very young children are often amoral because they haven't learned the difference between right and wrong yet. Immoral: अनैतिक / दुराचारी - Not conforming to accepted standards of morality. (Used for someone who knows the rules but intentionally breaks them). Example: Stealing from the poor is a highly immoral act.

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph. P. This demographic shift poses a significant challenge to the sustainability of pension systems and healthcare infrastructure. Q. Globally, the median age of the population is increasing as birth rates decline and life expectancy improves. R. Consequently, many nations are now reconsidering their retirement policies to encourage older citizens to remain in the workforce longer. S. As a result, the proportion of elderly individuals is rising at a rate faster than that of the working-age population.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct option is (a) - QSPR 📌 Explanation - To solve this, we look for the Opening Sentence and Logical Connectors: Opening Sentence: Q is the best starter because it introduces the broad global topic (increasing median age due to declining birth rates). Logical Pair (Q-S): S starts with "As a result," explaining the direct consequence of the trends mentioned in Q (fewer births + longer life = more elderly people). Logical Pair (S-P): P identifies the "demographic shift" (the rising proportion of elderly mentioned in S) and explains the specific "challenge" it creates for pensions and healthcare. Closing Sentence (R): R starts with "Consequently," offering the final solution or reaction to the problems described in P (changing retirement policies). Final Paragraph: Globally, the median age of the population is increasing as birth rates decline and life expectancy improves. As a result, the proportion of elderly individuals is rising at a rate faster than that of the working-age population. This demographic shift poses a significant challenge to the sustainability of pension systems and healthcare infrastructure. Consequently, many nations are now reconsidering their retirement policies to encourage older citizens to remain in the workforce longer.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of the given sentence. The claim that the system had been tampered with was dismissed as baseless.
options: The system tampered the claim, which was dismissed.
The dismissal of the system tampering was claimed.
It was dismissed as baseless that the system was tampered with.
Authorities dismissed the claim that someone had tampered with the system.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (d) 📌 Explanation - The original sentence is written in the Passive Voice ("The claim... was dismissed"). In English grammar, especially in competitive exams, a sentence is "improved" or "made sensibly correct" by identifying the logical performer of the action (the subject) or by ensuring the structure is clear and direct. Option (d) provides a logical Subject (Authorities) and uses the Active Voice (dismissed), which makes the sentence more direct and grammatically robust. It also correctly expands "tampered with" to "someone had tampered with," maintaining the tense and meaning perfectly.

questions.question: Change the narration of the given sentence. She said that they had already left before she arrived.
options: "They have already left before I arrived," she said
"They already left before I arrived," she said.
“They had already left before I arrived," she said
"They left before I had arrived," she said.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (c) 📌 Explanation - This is a question of Direct and Indirect Speech involving two past actions. In the original (Indirect) sentence, we have "had already left" (Past Perfect) and "arrived" (Simple Past). According to the rules of Narration, when a sentence in Direct Speech already uses the Past Perfect to show an action completed before another past action, the tense does not change when converting it to Indirect Speech. Grammatical Concept: The "No Change" Rule for Past Perfect When converting Direct to Indirect speech, we usually follow the "Backshift" rule (e.g., Simple Past becomes Past Perfect). However, if the Direct speech is already in the Past Perfect, it stays the same because there is no "further back" tense in English.

questions.question: To view history as a neutral record of events is to ignore the ___(1)___ nature of the archive itself. Every document preserved is an act of selection that simultaneously necessitates an act of erasure. In this sense, the archive does not merely store the past; it ___(2)___ it, molding raw memory into a narrative that serves the prevailing power structures of the present. Voices that lack the "grammars of legibility"—those of the marginalized or the defeated—are often ___(3)___ to the footnotes of history, rendered structurally unintelligible by the very tools meant to record them. This institutional ___(4)____ ensures that certain truths remain submerged, waiting for a radical archaeology to unearth them. Ultimately, the task of the modern historian is not just to recount what happened, but to ___(5)___ the silences, transforming the void of the unrecorded into a site of resistance and re-evaluation. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 1 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (b) 💢 Explanation - The sentence argues that history is not a "neutral record" because the archive involves selection and erasure. Therefore, the archive has a partisan nature, meaning it is biased or one-sided rather than objective. Why others are wrong: (a) benign: Means gentle or harmless. This contradicts the passage's tone regarding the "erasure" and power structures of archives. (c) redundant: Means no longer needed or useful. The archive is described as a powerful tool of selection, not something useless. (d) ephemeral: Means lasting for a very short time. Archives are meant for long-term preservation, making this contextually incorrect.

questions.question: To view history as a neutral record of events is to ignore the ___(1)___ nature of the archive itself. Every document preserved is an act of selection that simultaneously necessitates an act of erasure. In this sense, the archive does not merely store the past; it ___(2)___ it, molding raw memory into a narrative that serves the prevailing power structures of the present. Voices that lack the "grammars of legibility"—those of the marginalized or the defeated—are often ___(3)___ to the footnotes of history, rendered structurally unintelligible by the very tools meant to record them. This institutional ___(4)____ ensures that certain truths remain submerged, waiting for a radical archaeology to unearth them. Ultimately, the task of the modern historian is not just to recount what happened, but to ___(5)___ the silences, transforming the void of the unrecorded into a site of resistance and re-evaluation. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 2 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (b) 💢 Explanation - The text states the archive "molds" raw memory into a narrative. Mediates means to act as an intermediary or to influence/shape the process of how something is perceived. The archive doesn't just store; it filters and shapes the past. Why others are wrong: (a) authenticates: Means to prove something is true. The passage suggests the archive creates a specific (and potentially biased) narrative, not necessarily a "true" one. (c) obliterates: Means to destroy completely. While archives erase some things, the sentence says they "store" and "mold" the past, not just wipe it out. (d) replicates: Means to make an exact copy. The passage emphasizes that the archive changes and molds the memory, rather than copying it exactly.

questions.question: To view history as a neutral record of events is to ignore the ___(1)___ nature of the archive itself. Every document preserved is an act of selection that simultaneously necessitates an act of erasure. In this sense, the archive does not merely store the past; it ___(2)___ it, molding raw memory into a narrative that serves the prevailing power structures of the present. Voices that lack the "grammars of legibility"—those of the marginalized or the defeated—are often ___(3)___ to the footnotes of history, rendered structurally unintelligible by the very tools meant to record them. This institutional ___(4)____ ensures that certain truths remain submerged, waiting for a radical archaeology to unearth them. Ultimately, the task of the modern historian is not just to recount what happened, but to ___(5)___ the silences, transforming the void of the unrecorded into a site of resistance and re-evaluation. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 3 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (d) 💢 Explanation - To be relegated means to be assigned to a lower or less important rank or position. Marginalized voices are pushed to the "footnotes" (a lower position) because they are "unintelligible" to the system. Why others are wrong: (a) consecrated: Means to make or declare something sacred. This is the opposite of being ignored or marginalized. (c) elevated: Means to raise to a higher position. Footnotes represent a lower, less visible position, so "elevated" is logically incorrect. (b) assimilated: Means to take in and fully understand information or ideas. The passage says these voices are "unintelligible," meaning they are not understood or absorbed by the system.

questions.question: To view history as a neutral record of events is to ignore the ___(1)___ nature of the archive itself. Every document preserved is an act of selection that simultaneously necessitates an act of erasure. In this sense, the archive does not merely store the past; it ___(2)___ it, molding raw memory into a narrative that serves the prevailing power structures of the present. Voices that lack the "grammars of legibility"—those of the marginalized or the defeated—are often ___(3)___ to the footnotes of history, rendered structurally unintelligible by the very tools meant to record them. This institutional ___(4)____ ensures that certain truths remain submerged, waiting for a radical archaeology to unearth them. Ultimately, the task of the modern historian is not just to recount what happened, but to ___(5)___ the silences, transforming the void of the unrecorded into a site of resistance and re-evaluation. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 4 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (c) 💢 Explanation - In this philosophical context, foreclosure refers to the act of excluding something or preventing it from being considered. This "institutional foreclosure" ensures certain truths remain submerged and hidden from the record. Why others are wrong: (a) inertia: Means a tendency to do nothing or remain unchanged. While institutions have inertia, the passage is specifically about the active exclusion of truths. (b) transparency: Means being open and honest. The passage describes a system that hides and erases truths, which is the opposite of transparency. (d) exuberance: Means the quality of being full of energy and excitement. This is a positive, high-energy word that does not fit the serious tone of historical exclusion.

questions.question: To view history as a neutral record of events is to ignore the ___(1)___ nature of the archive itself. Every document preserved is an act of selection that simultaneously necessitates an act of erasure. In this sense, the archive does not merely store the past; it ___(2)___ it, molding raw memory into a narrative that serves the prevailing power structures of the present. Voices that lack the "grammars of legibility"—those of the marginalized or the defeated—are often ___(3)___ to the footnotes of history, rendered structurally unintelligible by the very tools meant to record them. This institutional ___(4)____ ensures that certain truths remain submerged, waiting for a radical archaeology to unearth them. Ultimately, the task of the modern historian is not just to recount what happened, but to ___(5)___ the silences, transforming the void of the unrecorded into a site of resistance and re-evaluation. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 5 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (d) 💢 Explanation - To interrogate means to ask questions closely or aggressively. The modern historian's task is to "interrogate the silences"—to question why things were left out and what those gaps mean. Why others are wrong: (a) commemorate: Means to recall and show respect for someone or something. While historians do this, the specific task here is to deal with "silences" (gaps/voids), which requires questioning (interrogating) rather than just celebrating. (b) overlook: Means to fail to notice. The historian's job is specifically to find what was ignored, not to ignore it further. (c) simplify: Means to make something easier to understand. The passage calls for "radical archaeology" and "re-evaluation," which implies a deep, complex investigation rather than simplification.

questions.question: Identify the word that is correctly spelt.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Resurgence Let’s see the meaning of all words: Pantheon - पंथन - a temple dedicated to all the gods Example: The Pantheon in Rome is a famous landmark. Accustomed - अभ्यस्त - familiar with or used to something Example: I'm accustomed to waking up early. Resurgence - पुनरुत्थान - the act of rising again or becoming popular again Example: There's been a resurgence of interest in vintage fashion. Pediatrician - बाल रोग विशेषज्ञ - a doctor who specializes in the care of children Example: The pediatrician examined the newborn baby.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence – The luscious chocolate cake was a rich, moist, and indulgent treat that melted in my mouth.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Delectable Let’s see the meaning of all words: Luscious - Delicious - रसपूर्ण - delicious and attractive Example: The luscious strawberries were sweet and juicy. Pharaoh - फारोह - ancient Egyptian ruler Example: The pharaoh ruled Egypt with an iron fist. Delectable - स्वादिष्ट - delicious and enjoyable Example: The delectable cake was devoured in minutes. Mediocre - मध्यम - average or ordinary Example: The movie was mediocre, nothing special. Satiate - संतुष्ट करना - to satisfy or fill Example: The meal was enough to satiate my hunger.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Stupefy
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Enlighten Let’s see the meaning of all words: Stupefy - मूर्ख बनाना - to make someone feel extremely surprised or shocked Example: The news stupefied everyone in the room. Exempt - छूट देना - to officially allow someone not to do something Example: He's exempt from physical education. Deflate - कम करना - to make something smaller or less Example: The tyre deflated slowly. Realm - क्षेत्र - a field or area of interest Example: Science fiction is outside my realm. Enlighten - ज्ञान देना - to give someone a clearer understanding Example: The teacher enlightened the students about the concept.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. Body and soul
questions.explanation: The correct answer is - With all your energy Let’s see the meaning of idiom: Body and soul - पूरी तरह से - with complete dedication or enthusiasm Example: She's a musician body and soul.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. Revel
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Carouse Let’s see the meaning of all words: Revel - आनंद मनाना - to enjoy yourself in a lively way Example: They reveled in the music festival. Carouse - शराब पीकर मजाक करना - to enjoy oneself with others, often drinking Example: They caroused till dawn. Yell - चिल्लाना - to shout loudly Example: She yelled for help. Scorn - अवमानना करना - to show contempt for someone Example: He scorned their efforts. Renewal - नवीकरण - the act of making something new again Example: The renewal of the contract was expected.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- The country's attempt to annex the disputed border region led to increased tensions with neighbouring nations and sparked a UN investigation into the matter.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Detach Let’s see the meaning of all words: Annex - अनुलिप्त करना - to attach or add something Example: The country annexed the neighbouring territory. Anile - पुराना - old Example: The anile policies needed reform. Detach - अलग करना - to separate Example: Detach the coupon and send it back. Abrogate - रद्द करना - to cancel Example: The government abrogated the treaty. Ornate - सजावटी - decorated Example: The ornate design was impressive.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. Evening prayer in church
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Vespers Let’s see the meaning of all words: Moor - दलदली भूमि - an area of wet, spongy ground Example: The moor was covered in fog. Vespers - सायंकालीन प्रार्थना - evening prayer in church Example: We attend vespers every evening. Petty - छोटा - small or unimportant Example: Petty issues were ignored. Regalia - राजसी वस्त्र - official clothes worn on special occasions Example: The king wore his regalia.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom. The entrepreneur's story is a classic tale of rags to riches, having built a billion-dollar company from scratch.
options: moving from a state of poverty to one of wealth and success
questions.explanation: The correct answer is - moving from a state of poverty to one of wealth and success Let’s see the meaning of idiom: Rags to riches - गरीबी से समृद्धि - moving from a state of poverty to one of wealth and success Example: Steve Jobs' life was a true story of rags to riches, co-founding Apple in a garage.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. Relating to eye
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Optique Let’s see the meaning of all words: Optique - दृष्टि संबंधी - relating to eye or vision Example: Optique exams are crucial for detecting eye issues. Hassle - झगड़ा - a difficult or annoying situation Example: Dealing with traffic is a hassle. Yoke - जोड़ना - a wooden beam used to join two animals Example: The oxen were joined by a yoke. Reflex - प्रतिक्षेप - an automatic response Example: The doctor tested her reflexes.

options: Neither conclusion I nor II follows
Both conclusions I and II follow
questions.explanation: • Some jasmines are roses. (FALSE) • All jasmines are roses. (FALSE)

questions.explanation: D is an immediate neighbor of E,

options: Flat No. 204, Emerald Heights, Malviya Nagar, Jaipur, Rajasthan – 302017
Flat No. 204, Emerald Heights, Malviya Nagar, Jaipur, Rajsthan – 302017
Flat No. 204, Emerald Hights, Malviya Nagar, Jaipur, Rajasthan - 302017
Flat No. 205, Emerald Heights, Malviya Nagar, Jaipur, Rajasthan – 302017
questions.explanation: Flat No. 204, Emerald Heights, Malviya Nagar, Jaipur, Rajasthan – 302017

questions.question: What comes next: 0, 6, 24, 60, 120, ?

Choose the assumptions that make this statement true. Choose the best option. Statement: Prakash decided to get his railway reservation done in the month of May for the journey he wants to undertake in July to Mumbai. Assumptions: • The railways open reservation two months in advance. • There is more than one train to Mumbai. • There will be a vacancy in the desired class.
questions.explanation: Prakash wants to go Mumbai in July, while he gets his railway reservation done in May It means that railways open reservation two months in advance. So, Assumption I is implicit. Assumptions II and III are not related to statement, so are not, implicit.

questions.question: Scenario: You are trying to find a specific book in a library. Which of the following is the most efficient first step?
options: Wander around randomly looking at all the shelves.
Start reading the titles of every book on one shelf.
Leave the library and try to find the book elsewhere.
questions.explanation: Librarians are trained to know the library’s organization system so ask a librarian for help is the most efficient first step.

questions.question: Convert (78)10 into Hexadecimal.
questions.explanation: In hexadecimal we write 10= a 13=d 11=b 14-e 12=c 15-f
